title: Practical Statistics for Data Scientists
subtitle: 50+ Essential Concepts Using R and Python
author: Peter Bruce
themes: Statistics is a foundational toolkit for data science, not a footnote
Resampling (bootstrap, permutation) is the modern path to inference
EDA is the most under-taught and highest-leverage data activity
Classical hypothesis testing is misused; effect sizes matter more than p-values
Regression is the gateway to most supervised learning
Tree-based methods (random forest, boosting) dominate tabular ML
Cross-language fluency: every concept implemented in both R and Python
awards: Top recommendation on the r/datascience reading lists
Widely adopted in data science bootcamps and corporate training
Cited as the practitioner companion to An Introduction to Statistical Learning
excerpt: Statistical methods are a key part of data science, yet few data scientists have formal statistical training. This book applies statistical concepts to data science from a practical standpoint, helping you avoid the misuse of statistical methods while teaching you the few that actually matter on the job.
